title: fraxinus excelsior var. oxyphylla
intro: Fraxinus excelsior var. oxyphylla, also known as the Ash-leaved Maple, is a deciduous tree native to Europe and western Asia. Its bark, leaves, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, respiratory problems, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Fraxinus excelsior var. oxyphylla include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Fraxinus excelsior var. oxyphylla can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
